scores.metric: full-build unmasked Levenshtein vs pristine text1b.o (clean pin-free body)

sessions.lever: pin-free clean body + 5 ff0/base ordering variants

reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is clean pure C: zero rules, zero asm, no pins/dead-stores/volatile. Not committable only because it does not byte-match (coupled s0/s1 register swap + delay-slot scheduling), not because it cheats.

remaining_gap.summary: Coupled register-allocation + scheduling tie in a 103-insn display-init function. Target: base(&D_800F7438) in $s0, ff0(0xF0) in $s1, with `li s1,240` scheduled into the func_80036EA8 jal delay slot. GCC: base in $s1, ff0 in $s0, with a nop in the jal delay slot and ff0 materialized later. The s0/s1 swap and the delay-slot fill appear coupled.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD matches via 5 register-asm pins (ff0 $17, temp_s3 $19, a0v $4, a1v $5). No pure-C lever flips the s0/s1 tie (5 measured).

next_hypotheses: decomp-permuter from the pin-free base (NOTE: 103 insns + many jal relocations -> isolated permuter scores are unreliable per register-alloc-pure-c; verify any find with full SHA1).
greg -da diagnosis to find which pseudo blocks $s0 for base; if it's the ff0-live-across-calls pseudo claiming $s0 first, a liveness reshape might flip it — none of 5 orderings did.
If walls: canonical-asm review (register rotation GCC 2.7.2 won't emit for this structure).

rejected_forms.form: base computed at top of function (base_top)
rejected_forms.reason: moves the lui s0 base materialization too early; worse
do not flip the s0/s1 tie or the jal-delay-slot fill
